item.title: JavaScript 数组方法 | JavaScript Array Methods
item.description:
卡洛斯·丹尼尔·奥尔特加·埃尔南德斯

发表于更改原始数组

  • 数组存取方法

    不修改原始数组。 创建新数组。

    • 理解简单 - 博主和程序员 - May 9 5月9日

Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Mahmoud EL-kariouny - May 1 5月1日



原文:

Carlos Daniel Ortega Hernandez

Posted on May 12

Changes original array

  • pop(): Removes the las element of an array and returns it. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.pop()); // 'Element 3'
console.log(array); // ['Element 1', 'Element 2'];
  • shift(): Removes the firts element of an array and returns it. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.shift()); // 'Element 1'
console.log(array); // ['Element 2', 'Element 3'];
  • push(): Adds a new element to the array, at the end of the array, and returns the new length of the array. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.push('Element 4')); // 4
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3', 'Element 4' ]
  • reverse(): Reverses the order of the elements in an array. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.reverse()); // [ 'Element 3', 'Element 2', 'Element 1' ]
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 3', 'Element 2', 'Element 1' ]
  • unshift(): Adds one or more elements to the beginning of an array, and returns the new length of the array. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
console.log(array.unshift('Element 4')); // 4
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 4', 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3' ]
  • sort(): Sorts the elements of an array. 
const array = [2, 8, 1, 4];
console.log(array.sort()); // [ 1, 2, 4, 8 ]
console.log(array); // [ 1, 2, 4, 8 ]
  • splice(): Changes the contents of an array, removing existing elements and/or adds new elements. 
const array = [2, 8, 1, 4, 'a', 'b'];
console.log(array.splice(1,3,'a')); // [ 8, 1, 4 ]
console.log(array); // [ 2, 'a', 'a', 'b' ]

Array Accessor Methods

Does not modify the original array. Creates new array.

  • join(): Joins all the elements of an array into a string and returns the string. 
const array = [2, 8, 1, 4, 'a', 'b', 'c']; console.log(array.join()); // '2,8,1,4,a,b,c'
console.log(array); // [ 2, 8, 1, 4, 'a', 'b', 'c' ]
  • slice(): Returns a part of the array in a new array. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3']; console.log(array.slice(1, 2)); // [ 'Element 2' ]
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3' ]
  • concat(): Joins two or more arrays into one. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3'];
const array2 = [1, 2, 3];
const array3 = [true, false]; console.log(array.concat(array2, array3)); // [ 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3', 1, 2, 3, true, false ]
console.log(array); // [ 'Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3' ]
  • includes(): Returns true if it finds the string, number, etc. or returns false if it finds nothing. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3']; console.log(array.includes(2)); // false
console.log(array.includes('Element 1')); // true
  • toString(): Returns all elements of the array, in a string. 
const array = ['Element 1', 'Element 2', 'Element 3']; console.log(array.toString()); // 'Element 1,Element 2,Element 3'
  • indexOf(): Returns the first index that matches the search. 
const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 3, 5, 2, 1]; console.log(array.indexOf(1)); // 0
  • lastIndexOf(): Returns the last index that matches the search. 
const array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 3, 5, 2, 1]; console.log(array.lastIndexOf(2)); // 7

Understanding Simplicity - Apr 28

Blogger and Programmer - May 9

Mahmoud EL-kariouny - May 1
item.author: carlos0318

item.title: 如何自动重新加载 node.js 服务器 | How to auto reload node.js server
item.description:
源码： https ://github.com/taowen/vite-howto/tree/main/packages/SSR/auto-reload-node-server

代码结构和动机

它是一个 node.js 应用程序，使用 express 监听http://localhost:3000

  • 当我们更改源时，开发服务器应该自动重新加载 node.js 服务器。 在不重新启动进程的情况下进行更改会很好。

    用户体验问题

    解决方案演练

    将 node.js 应用程序构建到包中

    服务器/vite.config.ts

     import { defineConfig } from ' vite ' export default defineConfig ({ build : { ssr : ' ./server-entry.ts ' , outDir : ' ../dist ' }, })

    将server/server-entry.ts到dist/server-entry.js及其引用的所有内容（node.js 标准库除外）。 它是 commonjs 格式，可以在 node.js 环境中执行。 开发服务器

    在开发过程中， http://localhost:3000/我们希望 vite 动态转换 server.ts，这样我们可以在进行更改后跳过编译过程

    import express from ' express ' ; import { createServer as createViteServer } from ' vite ' ; async function main () { const app = express () // auto reload in dev mode const vite = await createViteServer ({ server : { middlewareMode : ' ssr ' , watch : { // During tests we edit the files too fast and sometimes chokidar // misses change events, so enforce polling for consistency usePolling : true , interval : 100 } } }); app . all ( ' /(.*) ' , async ( req , resp ) => { req . url = req . originalUrl ; console . log ( req . method , req . url ); const { default : handle } = await vite . ssrLoadModule ( ' ./server/server.ts ' ); handle ( req , resp , ( e ) => { if ( e ) { vite . ssrFixStacktrace ( e ) console . error ( e . stack ) resp . status ( 500 ). end ( e . stack ); } else { resp . status ( 404 ). end (); } }); }) app . listen ( 3000 , () => { console . log ( ' http://localhost:3000 ' ) }); } main ();

    我们使用await vite.ssrLoadModule('./server/server.ts')来转换代码并运行它。 因为每个请求都会调用 ssrLoadModule，并且配置了server.watch

     const vite = await createViteServer ({ server : { middlewareMode : ' ssr ' , watch : { // During tests we edit the files too fast and sometimes chokidar // misses change events, so enforce polling for consistency usePolling : true , interval : 100 } } });

    如果我们更改服务器代码，我们只需刷新浏览器向开发服务器发送另一个请求即可看到效果。 vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e) will fix the exception stack trace, to report the correct original line number, instead of the line number in transformed file. vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e)将修复异常堆栈跟踪，以报告正确的原始行号，而不是转换文件中的行号。



原文:

Source code: https://github.com/taowen/vite-howto/tree/main/packages/SSR/auto-reload-node-server

Code Structure & Motivation

It is a node.js application, using express to listen at http://localhost:3000

  • server/server-entry.ts is the entry point, which listens the http port
  • server/server.ts is the main logic

DX Problems

dev server should auto reload the node.js server when we have changed the source. nodemon can monitor soure code change and restart node process, but it takes time to restart. It would be nice to make the change without process restart.

UX Problems

vite build server should package every server-entry.ts dependency (except node itself), so we do not need to npm install again when deploy.

Solution Walkthrough

build node.js application to a bundle

server/vite.config.ts

import { defineConfig } from 'vite' export default defineConfig({ build: { ssr: './server-entry.ts', outDir: '../dist' },
})

will bundle the server/server-entry.ts to dist/server-entry.js with everything it referenced (except node.js standard library). It is in commonjs format, ready to be executed in node.js environment. build.ssr is provided by vite to build node.js server.

development server

During development, http://localhost:3000/ we want vite to transform server.ts on the fly, so we can skip compilation process after making changes

import express from 'express';
import { createServer as createViteServer } from 'vite'; async function main() { const app = express() // auto reload in dev mode const vite = await createViteServer({ server: { middlewareMode: 'ssr', watch: { // During tests we edit the files too fast and sometimes chokidar // misses change events, so enforce polling for consistency usePolling: true, interval: 100 } } }); app.all('/(.*)', async (req, resp) => { req.url = req.originalUrl; console.log(req.method, req.url); const { default: handle } = await vite.ssrLoadModule('./server/server.ts'); handle(req, resp, (e) => { if (e) { vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e) console.error(e.stack) resp.status(500).end(e.stack); } else { resp.status(404).end(); } }); }) app.listen(3000, () => { console.log('http://localhost:3000') });
} main();

We use await vite.ssrLoadModule('./server/server.ts') to transform the code and run it. Because the ssrLoadModule invoked per request, and server.watch is configured

 const vite = await createViteServer({ server: { middlewareMode: 'ssr', watch: { // During tests we edit the files too fast and sometimes chokidar // misses change events, so enforce polling for consistency usePolling: true, interval: 100 } }
});

If we changed the server code, we can see the effect just by refreshing browser to send another request to dev server. vite.ssrFixStacktrace(e) will fix the exception stack trace, to report the correct original line number, instead of the line number in transformed file.

item.title: InfoAbout.me | InfoAbout.me
item.description:
这个应用程序的主要目的是让用户使用这个应用程序在各种社交媒体平台上查看自己上传的内容，这就是为什么应用程序被命名为InfoAbout.me的原因。

大多数时候，全球各地的人们必须在一天内访问各种应用程序或社交媒体平台，以检查他们在社交媒体平台上上传的内容，因此牢记这个问题，我制作了这个 Web 应用程序，人们可以在其中查看上传的内容他/她使用这个单一应用程序在 twitter 和 Dev 上发布的内容。

由于时间限制，我只集成了两个应用程序（Twitter + Dev）简单的功能，所以使用InfoAbout.me的用户只能看到他们上传的内容，不能对他们上传的内容进行任何操作。

将来这个应用程序将由我增强，并且像 auth2 等 appwrite 服务也将在其中实现。

目前，此应用程序允许用户使用 InfoAbout.me 登录，如果用户没有帐户，则用户需要创建一个帐户。

成功登录后，用户需要通过 Twitter 和 Dev 授权 InfoAbout.me 应用程序，以便 infoAbout.me 可以成功地从这些应用程序中获取用户数据。

提交类别：

链接到代码

其他资源/信息

[Note:] # (Be sure to link to any open source projects that are using your workflow!) [注意：] # （请务必链接到使用您的工作流程的任何开源项目！）

[Reminder]: # (Submissions are due on May 12th, 2022 @ 11:59 PM UTC). [提醒]：#（提交截止日期为 2022 年 5 月 12 日 @ 11:59 PM UTC）。



原文:

InfoAbout.me is an web application which provides convenience to users to check uploded contents by themselves on various social media platforms like Twitter and Dev.

The main motive of this application is to let the users to check their own uploaded contents on various social media platforms using this single application, that's why the application is named as InfoAbout.me.

Most of the time people all around the globe have to visit to various applications or social media platforms in a day to check what they have uploaded on social media platforms,so by keeping this problem in mind I made this web application where one can check uploaded contents by him/her on twitter and Dev using this single applcation.

Because of time limitation I integrated only two applications (Twitter + Dev) simple features in it, so using InfoAbout.me user can only see their uploaded contents, can't perform any operation with their uploaded contents.

In future this application will be enhanced by me and appwrite services like auth2 etc will also be implemented in it.

For now this application lets a user to login with InfoAbout.me, if user don't have an account then user will need to create an account.

After successfully Login user will need to authorize InfoAbout.me application with Twitter and Dev so that infoAbout.me can fetch User Data from these applications successfully.

Web2 Wizards

item.title: 如何在你的 nodeJS 应用程序中修复 openai 错误 | How to Fix error with openai in your nodeJS app
item.description:
凯欣德·阿德莱克

发表于当我遇到这个错误时，我正在尝试 openAI 的 api。

这很烦人。 为了解决这个问题，你将"type": "module"添加到你的 package.json 文件中。

如果您没有 package.json 文件，请使用 `npm init -y" 创建一个。

有了这个，你应该有这个：

这应该解决它。

但是......可能会出现其他问题。 由于您需要 .env 文件中的 API 密钥才能访问 api，因此您需要导入 dotenv 包。

在使用 npm 或 yarn 安装后，我使用了 require 关键字来导入它：

出于某种原因，这似乎引发了另一个错误：

这非常令人沮丧，堆栈溢出似乎没有直接的答案。

为了解决这个问题，将 require 更改为正常的 import 语句，

在这种情况下， import dotenv from 'dotenv/config'

最终代码如下所示：



原文:

Kehinde Adeleke

Posted on May 12

I was trying out openAI's api when I came across this error.

It was quite annoying. To solve it, you add the "type": "module" to your package.json file.

If you don't have a package.json file, use `npm init -y" to create one.

With that, you should have this:

which should solve it.

but...something else might come up. Since you need your API key in a .env file to access the api, you would need to import the dotenv package.

after installing with npm or yarn, I used the require keyword to import it:

`require('dotenv').config();

For some reason, that seems to throw another error:

It was very frustrating and stack overflow didn't seem to have a direct answer.

To solve it, change require to a normal import statement,

in this case it would be, import dotenv from 'dotenv/config'

the final code looks like this:

item.title: 只有垂直边距折叠 | Only vertical margins collapse
item.description:
当向 CSS 规范添加 margin-collapse 时，语言设计者做出了一个奇怪的选择：水平边距不应该折叠。

在早期，CSS 不打算用于布局。 编写规范的人想象的是标题和段落，而不是列和侧边栏。

所以这是我们的第一条规则：只有垂直边距折叠。

这是一个例子。 如果您使用的是桌面浏览器，请打开开发人员工具并自己检查边距：



原文:

1 Introduction of Margin Collapse in CSS 2 Only vertical margins collapse

When margin-collapse was added to the CSS specification, the language designers made a curious choice: horizontal margins shouldn't collapse.

In the early days, CSS wasn't intended to be used for layouts. The people writing the spec were imagining headings and paragraphs, not columns and sidebars.

So that's our first rule: only vertical margins collapse.

Here's an example. If you're using a desktop browser, pop open the developer tools and inspect the margins for yourself:

item.title: 在 MacOS 上加密文件夹 | Encrypting Folders on MacOS
item.description:
迈克尔·沃尔

发表于创建一个新文件夹，在我的示例中，该文件夹名为“secretstuff”。

  • 将您想要保护的任何文件、文件夹或内容复制到 secretstuff 文件夹中。

  • 启动磁盘工具，从文件菜单中选择新图像，从文件夹中选择图像。

  • 我选择了从上面的步骤 1 创建的文件夹。 将加密设置为 256 位 AES，并将图像格式设置为压缩。 选择保存。 出现提示时，提供一个长而强的密码，将密码存储在与文件夹和创建后的最终 dmg 文件分开的安全位置。

    1. 将创建一个新的secretstuff.dmg 文件，不要忘记删除源secretstuff 文件夹，但保留secretstuff.dmg 文件。

    2. 如果您想访问 secretstuff.dmg 文件的内容，您可以使用相同的磁盘工具工具，从文件菜单中选择打开磁盘映像，当提示时输入上面在步骤 4 中创建的密码。

    切尔西德弗罗 - 塞缪尔·欣奇利夫🚀 - May 5 5月5日

    Pachi 🪐 (she/her/ela) - Pachi 🪐 (她/她/ela) - May 3 5月3日



    原文:

    Michael Wahl

    Posted on May 11

    1. Create a new folder, for my example the folder is named "secretstuff".

    2. Copy whatever files, folders or content you want to protect into the secretstuff folder.

    3. Launch the Disk Utility, from the file menu, select new image, image from folder.

    4. I selected the folder I created from step 1 above. Set the encryption to 256-bit AES, and the image format to compressed. Select Save. When prompted, provide a long and strong password, store the password in a safe place separate from the folder and the final dmg file once created.

    1. A new secretstuff.dmg file will be created, dont forgot to delete the source secretstuff folder, but leave the secretstuff.dmg file.

    2. If you want to access the contents of the secretstuff.dmg file, you may do so by using the same Disk Utility tool, from the file menu, select open disk image, when prompted enter the password created above during step 4.

    Chelsea Devereaux - May 5

    Samuel Hinchliffe 🚀 - May 5

    Pachi 🪐 (she/her/ela) - May 3
item.author: 0xmichaelwahl

item.title: Pet and Vet 使用 AppWrite 为宠物打造的微型社交网络 | Pet and Vet a Micro Social Network for pets using AppWrite
item.description:
这个微社交网络的主要功能是：

  • 使用电子邮件注册/登录
  • 注册为兽医或宠物主人
  • 兽医可以输入 1 个或多个可以在地图上找到的联系地址
  • 宠物主人可以拥有宠物目录并决定是公开展示还是私下展示
  • 提示注册表，让每个人都知道感兴趣的信息
  • 通过 QR 分享您的宠物
  • 通过电子邮件或实时聊天与兽医联系

宠物和兽医建立在：

  • 不可知的 UI：用于组件的设计
  • 这个想法的灵感是创建一个项目，该项目将使用 AppWrite 提供的许多功能，同时成为如何连接对宠物感兴趣的人的示例。

    提交类别：

    链接到代码

    这个微社交网络的主要功能是：

    • 使用电子邮件注册/登录
    • 注册为兽医或宠物主人
    • 兽医可以输入 1 个或多个可以在地图上找到的联系地址
    • 宠物主人可以拥有宠物目录并决定是公开展示还是私下展示
    • 提示注册表，让每个人都知道感兴趣的信息
    • 通过 QR 分享您的宠物
    • 通过电子邮件或实时聊天与兽医联系

    介绍

    链接到演示文稿

    苗条的文档

    我们建议查看文档。

    后端

    APPWRITE_ENDPOINT=""

    APPWRITE_PROJECT=""

    其他资源/信息

    通过二维码分享活动

    寻找兽医并联系他们

    保留您的宠物目录

    宣传活动并与大家分享技巧

    随时更新您的个人信息

    作为兽医添加联系地址并与宠物主人实时聊天

    结论

    这次黑客马拉松让我了解了 Appwrite 以及它在不同场景下创建不同应用程序时提供的便利性，我个人认为 Appwrite 是一个新提案，也是创建生产质量应用程序的强大竞争对手



    原文:

    Pet and Vet is a micro social network with the purpose of connecting veterinarians with pet owners, as well as making your pet known to the world and learn about events in your country.

    The main functions of this micro social network are:

    • Register/Login with email
    • Registration as a Veterinarian or Pet Owner
    • Veterinarians can enter 1 or more contact addresses that can be located on a map
    • Pet owners can have a catalog of their pets and decide whether to display them publicly or privately
    • Registry of tips so that everyone knows information of interest
    • Share your pet via QR
    • Connect with a veterinarian via email or chat in real time

    Pet and Vet is built on:

    • AppWrite: as the backend that provides all the services
    • Gitpod: for remote development platform
    • Svelte: as a javascript framework
    • Agnostic UI: for the design of the components
    • Express JS: server-side sdk specific functionality

    The inspiration for the idea was to have a project that will use many of the features provided by AppWrite and at the same time be an example of how to connect people who are interested in pets.

    Submission Category:

    Web2 Wizards

    Link to Code

    Pet and Vet is a micro social network with the purpose of connecting veterinarians with pet owners, as well as making your pet known to the world and learn about events in your country.

    The main functions of this micro social network are:

    • Register/Login with email
    • Registration as a Veterinarian or Pet Owner
    • Veterinarians can enter 1 or more contact addresses that can be located on a map
    • Pet owners can have a catalog of their pets and decide whether to display them publicly or privately
    • Registry of tips so that everyone knows information of interest
    • Share your pet via QR
    • Connect with a veterinarian via email or chat in real time

    Pet and Vet is a project for the Hackaton AppWrite

    Link to presentation

    Svelte Docs

    We recommend to look at the documentation.

    Setup

    Backend

    Create a .env file and set yours keys

    APPWRITE_ENDPOINT=""

    APPWRITE_PROJECT=""

    …

    Additional Resources / Info

    Presentation

    Link to presentation

    Screenshots

    Show your pet to the world or keep it private

    Share the link of your pet on social networks

    Find events in your country

    Share events via QR code

    Find veterinarians and contact them

    Keep a catalog of your pets

    Promote events and share tips with everyone

    Update your personal information at any time

    As a veterinarian add contact addresses and chat in real time with pet owners

    Conclusions

    This hackathon allowed me to get to know Appwrite and the ease it provides when creating different applications in different scenarios, personally I see Appwrite as a new proposal and a strong competitor to create production quality applications

item.title: Kubernetes 1.24 发布：有什么新功能？ | Kubernetes 1.24 Released: What’s New?
item.description:
最新的 Kubernetes 版本 1.24 “Stargazer” 为开源系统带来了很多变化，用于自动化容器化应用程序的部署、扩展和管理。 现在有 14 项功能在测试阶段后永久可用，15 项创新处于测试阶段，13 项改进正在测试阶段。 此外，两个功能已被弃用，另外两个功能被永久删除。 此版本的主要主题包括：

观星者

根据其自己的说法，这个名称意指凝视星星可以激发科学探究和创造力，并有助于在未知领域进行导航。 标志的设计也富有想象力：它在星空前展示了一个风格化的望远镜。 图中所示的星座是昴宿星团，在希腊神话中也被称为七姐妹。 它向 Kubernetes 最初的内部项目名称致敬：Project 7。

在 1.20 版中被弃用后， Dockershim 组件现在已从 kubelet 中永久删除。

从 1.24 版开始，必须使用其他支持的运行时之一（例如 containerd 或 CRI-O），或者，如果您仍想依赖 Docker 引擎，则必须使用cri-dockerd 。 默认情况下，新的 beta API 不再处于活动状态

但是，现有的测试版 API 和现有测试版 API 的新版本仍将默认启用。 可以在GitHub 上找到有关此主题的更多信息。

它允许函数的调用者控制日志记录的所有方面，例如输出格式、详细程度、附加值和名称。

附加信息

有关进一步更新的信息，请访问 Kubernetes 网站。 完整的发行说明可以在 GitHub 上查看。 注册选项和程序可以在活动网站上找到。

还有一个 Kubernetes 入门的交互式教程。 如果您喜欢这篇博文，请考虑关注我以获得更多有趣的指南和教程。 🙂 Also, don't forget to buy me a coffee right here. 🙂另外，别忘了在这里给我买杯咖啡。



原文:

The latest Kubernetes version 1.24 “Stargazer” brings a lot of changes to the open-source system for automating the deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications. 14 features are now permanently available after the test phase, 15 innovations are in the beta phase and 13 improvements are being tested in the alpha phase. In addition, two features were deprecated and two others were permanently deleted. The main topics of this release include:

Stargazer

The Kubernetes development team came up with a special name for version 1.24: Stargazer. According to its own statement, this name is intended to refer to the fact that gazing at the stars can spark scientific inquiry and creativity and facilitate navigation in unknown areas. The logo is also imaginatively designed: it shows a stylized telescope in front of a starry sky. The constellation shown is the Pleiades, also known as the seven sisters in Greek mythology. It pays homage to the original internal project name of Kubernetes: Project 7.

Dockershim removed from kubelet

Dockershim was the CRI-compliant layer between Kubelet and the Docker daemon. After being deprecated in version 1.20, the Dockershim component has now been permanently deleted from the kubelet.

As of version 1.24, either one of the other supported runtimes (e.g. containerd or CRI-O) or, in case you still want to rely on Docker Engine, cri-dockerd must be used. Further information on precautions that may be necessary due to the removal of Dockershim is provided by Kubernetes in a guide.

New beta APIs are no longer active by default

New beta APIs are no longer enabled by default in Kubernetes. However, existing beta APIs and the new versions of existing beta APIs will still be on board by default. More on this topic can be found on GitHub.

Contextual logging in the alpha phase

The Contextual Logging feature was introduced with Kubernetes version 1.24. It allows a function’s caller to control all aspects of logging, such as output formatting, verbosity, additional values, ​​and names.

Additional Information

Information on further updates can be found on the Kubernetes website. The full release notes can be viewed on GitHub. The team behind the release of Kubernetes 1.24 will host a release webinar on May 24, 2022, from 9:45 am to 11:00 am Pacific Time. Registration options and the program can be found on the event website.

Kubernetes 1.24 is available for download on GitHub. There is also an interactive tutorial for getting started with Kubernetes. All release information is available in the Kubernetes Release Announcement.

If you liked this blog post, consider following me for more interesting guides and tutorials.🙂 Also, don’t forget to buy me a coffee right here.
item.author: talhakhalid101

item.title: 探索网络动画 | Exploring Web Animations
item.description:
我一直想知道动画是如何在网站上完成的。 我认为这会很酷，但我也怀疑我会实际使用多少。 我对可能的情况进行了简短的搜索，并找到了有关如何使单词变大变小的说明，以及如何使报纸矩形旋转并变小直到消失。 我打消了这个想法，认为我可能可以学到一些更实用的东西。

又有一天，我又给了动画一个镜头，偶然发现了这篇文章。 它展示了你可以做的非常酷的事情的例子：加载、欢迎、滚动、图像画廊和视觉反馈。 它还包括不同的库，这有助于弄清楚动画的来源是多么神秘。 我查找了更多库，发现更多：

有这么多图书馆，我不知所措。 有些似乎更倾向于做一件事，而另一些则是更大的图书馆，有更多的选择。

我查找了人们喜欢使用的库，发现 GSAP 提到了很多，所以我决定使用它。 它有很多功能，您还可以包含执行更具体任务的插件。 如果我学会了如何使用它，我似乎可以用这个库做很多不同的事情。 有很多文档包含代码示例、动画外观的视觉示例和 Youtube 教程视频。 他们还展示了人们创造的许多惊人的东西。

使用 GSAP

要包含 GSAP，请在包含 JavaScript 文件之前将此代码添加到您的 HTML 文件中：

 <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.10.4/gsap.min.js"></script> <script src="index.js"></script>

补间是 GSAP 的基础之一。 它基本上是一行代码，其中包含有关如何为对象设置动画的信息。 这是他们的补间示例：
gsap.to(".box", {rotation: 27, x: 100, duration: 1});

我将 GSAP 文档中的补间复制并粘贴到CodePen中，并尝试了代码的工作方式。 我在这里展示的每个示例都来自 GSAP 文档 JavaScript 示例。 我更改了一些内容以匹配我制作的 HTML。 这是我复制的第一个动画，即文本淡入淡出：

这是一个 .to() 方法。 持续时间是达到该不透明度所需的时间，因此不透明度从 1.0 开始到 0.3。 还有 .from() 和 .fromTo() 方法。 这是相同的示例，但使用了 .from() 方法。

持续时间是从该不透明度获取所需的时间，因此不透明度从 0.3 开始到 1.0。

这是水平和垂直基本运动的示例：

  • 向左移动：
    gsap.to("h1", {duration: 1, x: -100});

  • 向右移：
    gsap.to("h1", {duration: 1, x: 100});

  • 提升：
    gsap.to("h1", {duration: 1, y: -100});

  • 下移：
    gsap.to("h1", {duration: 1, y: 100});

这是一个如何使事物按顺序移动的示例。 在此示例中，无论 JavaScript 中的哪一行先出现，都会先移动：

放入属性时，它们必须在 camelCase 中。 例如，对于“font-size”，您可以输入“fontSize”。 这是一个具有许多属性的示例。 我玩弄了每个属性，试图弄清楚它们的作用。

 gsap.to("h1", 2, { scale: 0.1, y: 60, yoyo: true, repeat: -1, ease: "power1.inOut", delay:1, stagger: { amount: 1.5, grid: "auto", from: "center" } });

这使得文本 yoyo 从屏幕的左上角到右下角来回移动

  • 重复：-1 ：这使运动无限重复。 我重复了一个积极的1，而运动只重复了一次。
  • 有不同的选项，如弹性、反弹和正弦。 如果您向下滚动到“缓动”部分，则会有一个视觉显示，每个缓动如何使用图表显示每个缓动的移动速度。
  • 交错：交错的数量是文本在每个悠悠球之间停留的时间。 我不完全确定“网格”和“来自”在这一点上做了什么。

这是我为用作这篇文章的标题图像的变色背景 gif 所做的代码。

它创建从一种颜色到另一种颜色的一系列过渡，从 CSS 文件中的原始背景颜色开始。

期待

这些是学习如何制作有趣的可视化的第一步。 我们很快就会学到这一点，所以我也必须看看。 我将开始研究他们关于我应该知道的内容和使用他们的插件的更多建议。 最后，我还想扩展一下，看看如何使用其他库。



原文:

I've always wondered how animations are done on websites. I thought this would be so cool to learn, but I was also doubtful about how much of it I would actually use. I did a brief search at what was possible and found instructions on how to make words go big then small, and how to make a newspaper rectangle spin and get smaller until it's gone. I dismissed this idea thinking there is probably something more practical I can learn.

Another day, I gave animations another shot and came across this article. It showed examples of really cool things you can do: loading, welcoming, scrolling, image galleries, and visual feedback. It also included different libraries, which helped clear up how mysterious where animations come from are. I looked up more libraries and found even more:

There are so many libraries I am overwhelmed. Some seem like they were more oriented towards doing one thing while others were larger libraries with more options.

I looked up what library people prefer to use, and I found GSAP mentioned a lot so I decided to roll with it. It has a lot of capabilities, and you can also include plugins that do more specific tasks. It seems like I would be able to do a lot of different things with this library if I learned how to use it. There is a lot of documentation with examples of code, visual examples of how the animation looks, and Youtube tutorial videos. They also show a lot of amazing things people have created.

Using GSAP

“Animation ultimately boils down to changing property values many times per second, making something appear to move, fade, spin, etc. GSAP snags a starting value, an ending value and then interpolates between them 60 times per second.” -Getting Started with GSAP

To include GSAP, add this code to your HTML file before you include your JavaScript file:

<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.10.4/gsap.min.js"></script>
<script src="index.js"></script>

A tween is one of the foundations of GSAP. It is basically a line of code with information about how you want to animate an object. Here is their example of what a tween looks like:
gsap.to(".box", {rotation: 27, x: 100, duration: 1});

I copied and pasted tweens from GSAP documentation into CodePen, and played around with how the code worked. Every example I show here is from the GSAP documentation JavaScript examples. Some things I changed to match the HTML I made. Here is the first animation I copied, which is text fading:

Html:
<h1>Hello World</h1>

JavaScript:
gsap.to("h1", {duration: 5, opacity: 0.3});

This is a .to() method. The duration is how long it takes to get to that opacity so the opacity starts at 1.0 and goes to 0.3. There are also the .from() and .fromTo() methods. Here is the same example, but with the .from() method.

JavaScript:
gsap.from("h1", {duration: 5, opacity: 0.3});

The duration is how long it takes to get from that opacity so the opacity starts at 0.3 and goes to 1.0.

Here is an example for basic movement horizontally and vertically:

  • move left:
    gsap.to("h1", {duration: 1, x: -100});

  • move right:
    gsap.to("h1", {duration: 1, x: 100});

  • move up:
    gsap.to("h1", {duration: 1, y: -100});

  • move down:
    gsap.to("h1", {duration: 1, y: 100});

Here is an example of how to make things move in a sequence. Whichever line in the JavaScript comes first will move first in this example:

HTML:

<h1>First Movement</h1>
<h2>Second Movement</h2>
<h3>Third Movement</h3>

JavaScript:

var tl = gsap.timeline({repeat: 30, repeatDelay: 1});
tl.to("h1", {duration: 1, x: 200});
tl.to("h2", {duration: 1, x: 200, scale: 0.2});
tl.to("h3", {duration: 1, x: 200, scale: 2, y: 20});

When putting in properties, they have to be in camelCase. For example, for "font-size" you would put "fontSize". This is an example with many properties. I played around with each property trying to figure out what they do.

gsap.to("h1", 2, 
{
    scale: 0.1, 
    y: 60,
    yoyo: true, 
    repeat: -1, 
    ease: "power1.inOut",
    delay:1,
    stagger: 
    {
      amount: 1.5, 
      grid: "auto",
      from: "center"
    }
 });

This makes the text yoyo back and forth from the top left towards the bottom right of the screen

  • gsap.to("h1", 2 : The 2 is the duration. Apparently, you don't need to specify "duration" here.
  • scale: 0.1 : The scale is the size of the text.
  • yoyo: true : When I made yoyo false, the text did not move back to where it initially started.
  • repeat: -1 : This makes the movement repeat infinitely. I made repeat a positive 1, and the movement only repeated one time.
  • ease: "power1.inOut" : The ease is the style of the movement. There are different options like elastic, bounce, and sine. There is a visual display if you scroll down to the "Easing" section of how each ease works with a graph of how fast the movement is for each.
  • delay:1 : Delay is how long until the movement starts.
  • stagger : The amount of stagger is how long the text stays at that point in between each yoyo. I'm not entirely sure what the "grid" and "from" do at this point.

Here is the code I made for the color changing background gif I used as the header image for this post.

CSS:
background-color: #F8D6FF;

JavaScript:

ar tl = gsap.timeline({repeat: 3});

tl.to("h1", 
{
  duration: 1, 
  backgroundColor: "#F8D6FF"
});

tl.to("h1", 
{
  duration: 2,
  backgroundColor: "#D8C3E8"
});

tl.to("h1", 
{
  duration: 2,
  backgroundColor: "#E9E1FE"
});

tl.to("h1", 
{
  duration: 2,
  backgroundColor: "#C3C3E8"
});

tl.to("h1", 
{
  duration: 2,
  backgroundColor: "#D6E1FF"
});

tl.to("h1", 
{
  duration: 2, 
  backgroundColor: "#F8D6FF"
});

It creates a sequence of transitions from one color to the next, starting with the original background color from the CSS file.

Looking Forward

These are the first steps to learning how to make fun visualizations. GSAP also has information on how to incorporate it in React. We will be learning that soon so I will have to take a look at that as well. I'm going to start looking into more of their recommendations on what I should know and utilizing their plugins. Eventually, I'd also like to branch out and see how to use other libraries.

item.title: Next.js 中的 Markdoc 入门 | Getting started with Markdoc in Next.js
item.description:
无论是用于简单的静态网站、创作工具还是成熟的文档网站，Markdoc 都旨在与您一起成长，因此无论您的文档变得多么庞大或复杂，您都可以创建引人入胜的体验。 让我们来看看如何开始。
本教程的其余部分在 Next.js 中使用create-next-app实现。

安装

要开始使用 Markdoc，您首先需要安装它：

 npm install @markdoc/markdoc --save

或者

yarn add @markdoc/markdoc –-save

由于此示例应用程序使用 Next.js，因此您还需要安装 Next.js 插件：

 npm install @markdoc/next.js --save

最后，将以下行添加到 next.config.js 文件中：

 const withMarkdoc = require ( " @markdoc/next.js " ); module . exports = withMarkdoc ()({ pageExtensions : [ " js " , " md " , " mdoc " ], });

这些行允许您使用.js 、 .md和.mdoc文件。

配置文件的这些添加使您能够以 JavaScript 或 Markdown 文件格式编写文档。 如果您想将 Markdown 文件与 Next.js 以外的其他框架一起使用，则需要一个插件。

既然都设置好了，让我们写一些内容。

使用 Markdoc

首先，使用create-next-app找到启动 Next.js 应用程序时自动生成的pages文件夹，并在其中创建一个新的index.md文件。

对于这些，您必须在 JavaScript 中定义您的内容并使用Markdoc.transform()和Markdoc.renderers.react()来渲染所有内容。
让我们看看如何做到这一点。

变量

变量在配置对象中定义，然后可以在您的内容中使用。 在我们分解并解释不同部分之前，这是一个完整的代码示例的样子。

 // config.js export const config = { variables : { user : { name : " Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson " , }, }, };
 // Page.js import React from " react " ; import Markdoc from " @markdoc/markdoc " ; import { config } from " ./config.js " ; const Page = () => { const doc = ` Hello {% $user.name %} ` ; const content = Markdoc . transform ( doc , config ); return < section > { Markdoc . renderers . react ( content , React )} < /section> ; }; export default Page ;

例如，如果你想显示一个用户的名字，你可以像这样声明一个配置对象：

 const config = { variables : { user : { name : ' Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson ' } } };

在 Markdoc 中，要引用配置中的变量，请在变量名称前加上 $dollarSign。 例如，您可以这样引用用户名：

 const content = ` Hello {% $user.name %} ` ;

不要忘记在变量前面加上 $，否则它将被解释为标签。

然后，您需要在Markdoc.transform()中传递这两个变量并使用Markdoc.renderers.react()呈现您的内容。

 const content = Markdoc . transform ( doc , config ); return < section > { Markdoc . renderers . react ( content , React )} < /section> ;

使用变量是一项强大的功能，例如，如果您想显示动态数据，例如用户的 API 密钥。 可以在Stripe 文档网站上找到此类功能的示例。

自定义样式

例如，您可以使用以下语法添加 ID 和类：

 // index.md # My title {% #custom-id %} # Another title {% .custom-class-name-here %}

然后，您可以在 CSS 中引用这些来应用样式。

 // styles .css #custom-id { color : purple ; } .custom-class-name-here { color : darkblue ; }

这将生成以下 HTML：

 <h1 id= "custom-id" > My title </h1> <h1 class= "custom-class-name-here" > Another title </h1>

并渲染如下所示的内容：

一些与样式相关的属性也可以使用注释来应用：

 Function {% width="25%" %} Example {% align="right" %}

标签

例如，您可以尝试使用if标记来有条件地呈现内容。

 const config = { variables : { tags : { featureFlagEnabled : true , }, }, }; const document = ` {% if $tags.featureFlagEnabled %} Here's some secret content {% /if %} ` ;

您可以通过三个步骤构建自己的标签。 让我们看一个带有自定义横幅组件的示例。 首先，您需要从创建要渲染的 React 组件开始。 小型横幅组件的代码可能如下所示： 

 // Banner.js const Banner = ({ type , children }) => { return ( < section className = { `banner ${ type } ` } > { children } < style jsx > { ` .alert { border: 1px solid red; } ` } < /style > < /section > ); }; export default Banner ;

该组件将接受type属性来指示横幅是否应设置为警报、信息、警告横幅等样式。 children属性表示稍后将在 Markdown 文件中的该标记内传递的内容。

为了能够将此组件用作 Markdown 文件中的标签，首先在 Next.js 应用程序的根目录下创建一个“markdoc”文件夹，并在其中创建一个“tags.js”文件，该文件将包含您的所有自定义标签.

这个应用程序的架构最终看起来像这样：

 components/ |-- Banner.js markdoc/ |-- tags.js pages/ index.md

在您的自定义标签文件（此处tags.js ）中，您需要导入您的 React 组件并导出一个包含您要显示的组件的变量。 您还将包括您想要使用的任何属性。 在这种情况下，横幅的类型。

在声明属性时，您需要指定它们的数据类型。

 // markdoc/tags.js import Banner from " ../components/Banner " ; export const banner = { Component : Banner , attributes : { type : { type : String , }, }, };

最后一步是在您的 Markdown 内容中使用此自定义标签，如下所示：

 {% banner type="alert" %} This is an alert banner {% /banner %}

如果您创建一个不接受任何子标签的自定义标签，则可以将其编写为自闭合标签：

语法验证

此外，Markdoc 允许您验证在渲染之前生成的抽象语法树 (AST)。 如果您考虑上面编写的Banner组件，您可以在用 JavaScript 编写内容时将其用作标记，并在呈现之前检查任何语法错误。

例如，如果在没有所需type属性的情况下使用横幅标签，您可以实现一些错误处理以避免呈现损坏的内容。
这种语法验证可以使用Markdoc.validate()用一行来实现。

 const config = { tags : { banner , }, }; const content = ` {% banner %} Example banner with a syntax error {% /banner %} ` ; const ast = Markdoc . parse ( content ); const errors = Markdoc . validate ( ast , config ); // Handle errors

在这种情况下，返回的错误将如下所示。

职能

您可以通过编写自己的函数来使用自定义实用程序扩展 Markdoc。 例如，如果您想添加一种将内容转换为大写的方法，您可以首先在您的markdoc文件夹中创建一个文件，例如functions.js 。 在此文件中，添加以下帮助函数：

 // markdoc/functions.js export const uppercase = { transform ( parameters ) { const string = parameters [ 0 ]; return typeof string === ' string ' ? string . toUpperCase () : string ; } };

然后在需要它的组件中导入这个函数，并在你的配置对象中传递它：

 import { uppercase } from " ../markdoc/functions " ; const config = { functions : { uppercase } };

在{% %}标签内的内容中调用它：

 const document = ` {% uppercase("Hello, World") %} `

最后，调用Markdoc.transform()并使用 React 渲染器渲染所有内容。

 const doc = Markdoc . transform ( document , config ); Markdoc . renderers . react ( doc , React );

因此，一个小组件的完整语法如下所示：

 // config.js export const config = { functions : { uppercase }, };
 // functions.js export const uppercase = { transform ( parameters ) { const string = parameters [ 0 ]; return typeof string === " string " ? string . toUpperCase () : string ; }, };
 // Page.js import Markdoc from " @markdoc/markdoc " ; import { config } from " ./config.js " ; import { uppercase } from " ./functions.js " ; const Page = () => { const document = ` {% uppercase("Hello, World") %} ` ; const doc = Markdoc . transform ( document , config ); return < section > { Markdoc . renderers . react ( doc , React )} < /section> ; }; export default Page ;

一些内置函数可用，例如equals ，如果两个变量相等，则显示一些内容， not/and/or在if语句中使用以执行布尔运算，如果第一个返回未定义，则default返回传递的第二个参数，并debug将值序列化为 JSON 以在调试时为您提供帮助。

资源

如果您有兴趣了解更多信息，可以查看以下一些有用的资源，如果您有兴趣，我们欢迎您对存储库做出贡献！ 我们迫不及待地想看看您将使用 Markdoc 构建什么，并毫不犹豫地告诉我们您的想法！

马克多克游乐场
保持联系

此外，您可以通过以下几种方式了解 Stripe 的最新信息：
📣 Follow us on Twitter 📣 在Twitter 上关注我们
💬 Join the official Discord server 💬 加入Discord 官方服务器
📺 Subscribe to our Youtube channel 📺 订阅我们的Youtube 频道
📧 Sign up for the Dev Digest 📧 注册开发文摘



原文:

Stripe is open-sourcing Markdoc, the Markdown-based authoring system that powers the Stripe documentation website. Whether it’s for a simple static site, authoring tooling, or a full-blown documentation website, Markdoc is designed to grow with you, so you can create engaging experiences no matter how large or complex your documentation becomes. Let’s have a look at how to get started.
The rest of this tutorial is implemented in Next.js, using create-next-app.

Installation

To start using Markdoc, you first need to install it with:

npm install @markdoc/markdoc --save

or

yarn add @markdoc/markdoc –-save

As this sample app is using Next.js, you also need to install the Next.js plugin:

npm install @markdoc/next.js --save

Finally, add the following lines to the next.config.js file:

const withMarkdoc = require("@markdoc/next.js");
module.exports = withMarkdoc()({ pageExtensions: ["js", "md", "mdoc"],
});

These lines allow you to use .js, .md and .mdoc files.

These additions to the config file enable you to write your docs in either JavaScript or Markdown file formats. If you would like to use Markdown files with another framework than Next.js, a plugin will be required.

Now that you’re all set up, let’s write some content.

Using Markdoc

To get started, locate the pages folder that’s automatically generated when spinning up a Next.js app with create-next-app, and create a new index.md file inside it.

Markdoc syntax is a superset of Markdown, specifically the CommonMark specification, so, in this file, you can write content using this syntax.

# Some title
## A subtitle
This is a paragraph with a [link to your awesome website](https://your-awesome-website.com)

Markdoc is extensible so you can also use variables, functions, and create custom tags. For these, you have to define your content in JavaScript and use Markdoc.transform() and Markdoc.renderers.react() to render everything.
Let’s look into how to do that.

Variables

Variables are defined in a config object and can then be used in your content. Here’s what a complete code sample could look like before we break it down and explain the different pieces.

// config.js
export const config = { variables: { user: { name: "Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson", }, },
};

// Page.js
import React from "react";
import Markdoc from "@markdoc/markdoc";
import { config } from "./config.js"; const Page = () => { const doc = ` Hello {% $user.name %} `; const content= Markdoc.transform(doc, config); return <section>{Markdoc.renderers.react(content, React)}</section>;
}; export default Page;

For example, if you wanted to display a user’s name, you would declare a config object like this:

 const config = { variables: { user: { name: 'Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson' } } };

In Markdoc, to reference the variables in your config, prepend the variable name with a $dollarSign. For example, you would refer to the user’s name like this:

const content = `
Hello {% $user.name %}
`;

Don’t forget to prepend the variable with a $, otherwise it will be interpreted as a tag.

Then, you need to pass these two variables in Markdoc.transform() and render your content, using Markdoc.renderers.react().

const content= Markdoc.transform(doc, config); return <section>{Markdoc.renderers.react(content, React)}</section>;

Using variables is a powerful feature, for instance, if you want to display dynamic data, such as a user’s API key. An example of such a feature can be found on the Stripe documentation website.

Customizing styles

Markdoc introduces the concept of annotations to allow you to style different nodes, which are elements Markdoc inherits from Markdown.

For example, you can add IDs and classes with the following syntax:

// index.md
# My title {% #custom-id %}
# Another title {% .custom-class-name-here %}

You can then refer to these in your CSS to apply styles.

// styles.css
#custom-id { color: purple;
}
.custom-class-name-here { color: darkblue;
}

This would generate the following HTML:

<h1 id="custom-id">My title </h1>
<h1 class="custom-class-name-here">Another title </h1>

And render the content shown below:

Some style-related attributes can also be applied using annotations:

Function {% width="25%" %}
Example  {% align="right" %}

Tags

Markdoc comes with four built-in tags and also lets you build your own. For example, you can try the if tag that lets you conditionally render content.

const config = { variables: { tags: { featureFlagEnabled: true, }, },
}; const document = `
{% if $tags.featureFlagEnabled %} Here's some secret content {% /if %}
`;

You can build your own tags in three steps. Let’s go through an example with a custom banner component. First, you need to start by creating the React component that you want to render. The code for a small banner component could look like this:

// Banner.js
const Banner = ({ type, children }) => { return ( <section className={`banner ${type}`}> {children} <style jsx>{` .alert { border: 1px solid red; } `}</style>
 </section>
 );
}; export default Banner;

This component will accept a type prop to indicate if the banner should be styled as an alert, info, warning banner, etc. The children prop represents the content that will be passed inside that tag in the Markdown file later on.

To be able to use this component as a tag in a Markdown file, first create a “markdoc” folder at the root of your Next.js app, and a “tags.js” file inside it, that will contain all your custom tags.

This app’s architecture would end up looking like this:

components/
|-- Banner.js
markdoc/
|-- tags.js
pages/
   index.md

Inside your custom tag file (here tags.js), you need to import your React component and export a variable containing the component you want to display. You would also include any attributes you want to use. In this case, the type of banner.

When declaring the attributes, you need to specify their data type.

// markdoc/tags.js
import Banner from "../components/Banner"; export const banner = { Component: Banner, attributes: { type: { type: String, }, },
};

The final step is to use this custom tag in your Markdown content, like this:

{% banner type="alert" %}
This is an alert banner
{% /banner %}

If you create a custom tag that does not accept any children, you can write it as a self-closing tag:

Syntax validation

Additionally, Markdoc lets you validate the abstract syntax tree (AST) generated before rendering. If you consider the Banner component written above, you can use it as a tag when writing your content in JavaScript and check for any syntax error before rendering.

For example, if a banner tag is used without a type attribute that is required, you can implement some error handling to avoid rendering broken content.
This syntax validation can be implemented with a single line, using Markdoc.validate().

 const config = { tags: { banner, }, }; const content = ` {% banner %} Example banner with a syntax error {% /banner %}
`; const ast = Markdoc.parse(content); const errors = Markdoc.validate(ast, config); // Handle errors

In this case, the error returned will look like this.

Functions

You can extend Markdoc with custom utilities by writing your own functions. For example, if you wanted to add a way to transform your content to uppercase, you would start by creating a file inside your markdoc folder, for example functions.js. In this file, add the following helper function:

// markdoc/functions.js export const uppercase = { transform(parameters) { const string = parameters[0]; return typeof string === 'string' ? string.toUpperCase() : string; }
};

Then import this function in the component that needs it, and pass it in your config object:

import { uppercase } from "../markdoc/functions"; const config = { functions: { uppercase }
};

Call it in your content within {% %} tags:

const document = ` {% uppercase("Hello, World") %}
`

And finally, call Markdoc.transform() and use the React renderer to render everything.

const doc = Markdoc.transform(document, config);
Markdoc.renderers.react(doc, React);

So, the complete syntax for a small component would look like this:

// config.js
export const config = { functions: { uppercase },
};

// functions.js
export const uppercase = { transform(parameters) { const string = parameters[0]; return typeof string === "string" ? string.toUpperCase() : string; }, };

// Page.js
import Markdoc from "@markdoc/markdoc";
import { config } from "./config.js";
import { uppercase } from "./functions.js"; const Page = () => { const document = ` {% uppercase("Hello, World") %} `; const doc = Markdoc.transform(document, config); return <section>{Markdoc.renderers.react(doc, React)}</section>;
}; export default Page;

Some built-in functions are available such as equals, to display some content if two variables are equal, not/and/or to use in an if statement to perform boolean operations, default that returns the second parameter passed if the first returns undefined, and debug that serializes the values as JSON to help you while debugging.

Resources

If you’re interested in learning more, here are some useful resources you can check out, and if you’re interested, we welcome your contributions to the repository! We can’t wait to see what you’re going to build with Markdoc and don’t hesitate to let us know what you think!

Official Markdoc documentation
Markdoc repository
Markdoc Next.js plugin repository
Markdoc playground
Next.js boilerplate demo

Stay connected

In addition, you can stay up to date with Stripe in a few ways:
📣 Follow us on Twitter
💬 Join the official Discord server
📺 Subscribe to our Youtube channel
📧 Sign up for the Dev Digest

item.title: 不要手动解析 CSV！ | Don't parse CSVs by hand!
item.description:
我已经多次看到用于解析 CSV 数据的自定义 JavaScript 代码。 它看起来像这样：

 const csvData = fs . readFileSync ( csvFilePath , ' utf8 ' ); const lines = csvData . split ( ' \n ' ); const rows = lines . map ( line => line . split ( ' , ' ));

如此简单，如果您正在解析一个您知道其内容的静态 CSV 文件，这可能会很好。 但是，如果您正在解析未知的 CSV 文件，请不要这样做，它可能会以多种方式破坏。

会出什么问题

逃逸的细胞

这些列通常用引号转义。 具有此类列的行的原始数据可能如下所示：

 Name,Best Line Hamlet,"To be, or not to be"

尽管第二行有两个逗号，但这会生成一个包含两列而不是三列的表：

姓名最佳线路
村庄生存还是毁灭

这打破了简单的 CSV 解析器实现，它只是在,上拆分。

行尾

简单的 CSV 解析器实现假定 LF 行结尾 ( \n )，但在 Windows 上创建的文本文件，包括 CSV 文件，通常会有 CRLF 行结尾 ( \r\n )。 此外，在转义的单元格中可能存在行尾字符。 不用说，这些案例破坏了简单的 CSV 解析器实现。

该怎么做

引入为此而构建的库。 我喜欢使用csv-parse 。 它易于使用：

 import { parse as csvParse } from ' csv-parse/sync ' ; const csvData = fs . readFileSync ( csvFilePath , ' utf8 ' ); const rows = csvParse ( csvData );

就是这样。

结论

我知道没有人喜欢引入库来解决看似简单的任务，但正确解析任意 CSV 实际上比乍一看要困难得多。 省去自己的痛苦，使用别人久经考验的解析器。



原文:

I've seen custom JavaScript code to parse CSV data a number of times. It looks something like this:

const csvData = fs.readFileSync(csvFilePath, 'utf8');
const lines = csvData.split('\n');
const rows = lines.map(line => line.split(','));

So simple, and it may be fine if you're parsing a single static CSV file whose content you know. But if you're parsing unknown CSV files, don't do this, there are many ways it can break.

What Can Go Wrong

Escaped cells

Columns in a CSV file may contain commas. Such columns are typically escaped with quotes. The raw data for a row with such a column might look something like this:

Name,Best Line
Hamlet,"To be, or not to be"

Though the second line has two commas, this produces a table with two columns, not three:

Name Best Line
Hamlet To be, or not to be

This breaks the simple CSV parser implementation which just splits on ,.

Line Endings

The simple CSV parser implementation assumes LF line endings (\n), but text files, including CSV files, created on Windows will often have CRLF line endings (\r\n). Further, there may be line ending characters within escaped cells. Needless to say, these cases break the simple CSV parser implementation.

What to do instead

Bring in a library that's built for this. I like to use csv-parse. It's easy to use:

import { parse as csvParse } from 'csv-parse/sync'; const csvData = fs.readFileSync(csvFilePath, 'utf8');
const rows = csvParse(csvData);

That's it.

Conclusion

I know no one likes bringing in libraries to solve what seems to be a simple task, but parsing arbitrary CSVs correctly is actually a lot harder than it looks at first glance. Save yourself the pain, use someone else's battle-hardened parser.

item.title: 想成功吗？ 做一只无所畏惧的老鼠| 应用种子 | Want to succeed? Be a fearless rat | AppSeed
item.description:
你好编码员！

通常，我写的是自动化和seed projects 。 好吧，这次的帖子是关于在yTube上发布的一个虚拟视频，它帮助我在困难时期继续我的创业。 提前致谢！

对上述视频中使用的语言感到非常抱歉，但内容突出了一些非常重要的事实：

  • Don't take it as granted what society deliver
    • 困难的时候，想象you're the rat （对我来说有效）。
      • 我对任何人的建议？ be the rat!

博主和程序员 - Apr 24 4月24日



原文:

Hello coders!

Usually, I'm writing about automation and seed projects. Well, this time the post is about a dummy video published on yTube that helps me to keep going with my startUp during the hard times. Thanks in advance!

Really sorry for the language used in the mentioned video, but the content highlights a few really important facts:

  • Don't take it as granted what society deliver
    • Video facts: the rat goes straight to the cats gang
  • Listen to your inner calls
    • Video facts: The rat feels or thinks that his road is forward, not aside.
  • When is hard, imagine that you're the rat (for me works).
    • Video facts: The rat do whatever is required, based on his knowledge and abilities, to reach his goal flawlessly.

My advice to anyone? be the rat!

Blogger and Programmer - Apr 24
